options.C: By configuring it in the compile()  method
options.D: By adding an instance of keras.callbacks.Dropout  as a callback in the fit()  method

options.A: The model is too strongly regularised. Reduce or remove regularisation.

questions.question_text: In the context of deep learning models, what is a latent space?

options.A: A space where data is stored before being processed by the model
options.B: A memory buffer used for storing training data
options.C: A lower-dimensional representation of input data, learned by the model
options.D: The resulting vector space after performing one-hot-encoding of categorical features

questions.question_text: What number of dimensions consitute valid input to an RNN layer, such as keras.layers.LSTM?

options.B: 2 (the batch dimension and the time dimension)
options.C: 3 (the batch dimension, the time dimension, and the feature dimension)
options.D: Any dimension greater or equal to 1 is valid

options.A: Use a saturating activation function, such as sigmoid or tanh
options.B: Apply L2 regularisation to the network weights
options.D: Add pooling layers to downsample the intermediate representations

questions.question_text: A common approach to deploying a LLM-based chatbot application is to separate it into a frontend server that displays the application, and a backend server that runs the language model. The two communicate via a REST API. What are two (2) benefits of this approach? Select both correct answers.

options.A: Communication over a REST API ensures low latency
options.B: The computational load of running the LLM can be split between the two servers
options.C: We can switch between different models without downtime
options.D: The backend server can continuously retrain the LLM as new data comes is
options.E: We can scale the capacity of the two servers independently
options.F: Communication between the frontend and backend server is end-to-end (E2E) encrypted, which preserves privacy for the user

questions.question_text: Explain the self-attention mechanism, and how it is used in Transformer-type language models.
questions.model_answer: Answers should at minimum state that self-attention is a way to compute relations between words in a sentence, and that these are used in Transformers to infer (semantic) meaning from context. Better answers include that the attention score between two tokens is computed by taking the dot product between the (embedded) token vectors, and that the attention scores are used as weights to create a new context-aware vector for each token position. Ideally the answer should also include the generalisation into the key-query-value model token vectors are processed by different Dense layers before attention is computed, forming an attention head. Writing the attention mechanism in equation form is not expected.

questions.question_text: Select one (1) of the example tasks below, and motivate why deep learning is a good choice for solving this task. Recommend a type of model architecture that would be suitable, and explain how you would try to find the optimal model complexity — so that it is big enough to perform well, but not so big it overfits on the training data. • Image classification • Time series forecasting • Text classification (i.e. sentiment analysis)
questions.model_answer: Here 1 point is assigned to each of the three parts of the question -- one for motivating DL as a solution, one for identifying a suitable model, and one for explaining how to find the optimal complexity. There is no true answer to either, so emphasis is given to showing reasoning based on knowledge from the course. We expect an answer on the first part to include an explanation on how DL can identify patterns at different spatial and/or temporal hierarchies, and do automatic feature engineering. The third part is more of a general ML question and must include something about evaluating models on a statistically independent dataset.
